import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut state = 0
  mut cur = 0
  mut neg = false
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  while i <= n
    mut c = 32
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(v)
        c = v
      when None
        c = 32
      end
    end

    let is_digit = c >= 48 and c <= 57
    let is_minus = c == 45

    if state == 0
      if is_digit
        cur = c - 48
        neg = false
        state = 2
      else if is_minus
        neg = true
        state = 1
      end
    else if state == 1
      if is_digit
        cur = c - 48
        state = 2
      else if is_minus
        neg = true
      else
        state = 0
        neg = false
      end
    else
      if is_digit
        cur = cur * 10 + (c - 48)
      else
        if neg
          result = list.push(result, -cur)
        else
          result = list.push(result, cur)
        end
        state = 0
        neg = false
        cur = 0
        if is_minus
          neg = true
          state = 1
        end
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  let thresholds = parse_ints(line2)
  let t = match thresholds.get(0)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end

  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  for x in nums
    if x > t
      if first
        out = "#{x}"
        first = false
      else
        out = "#{out} #{x}"
      end
    end
  end
  println(out)
end
